comments.body: Change LGTM, but Red Hat CI doesn't seem happy with  buildah-containers.md for some reason again.

:pushpin: Commit dea97a9 has been approved by `@ripcurld0`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=@ripcurld0 dea97a9e6e08e55fd102a2e7d5bcdeecad8313a8 -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.

Packages available in Fedora 
https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2018-e821bfc2b2
Please test and update karma, if you have the time.

Hi @rhatdan I would love that.
Any particular document that I should read before testing it or should I be creative?
In addition, how do I update karma?

Thanks.

Seems like we have a small problem with [`/usr/share/bash-completion/completions/buildah`](https://taskotron.fedoraproject.org/artifacts/all/0bda1604-fb8e-11e7-96fd-525400fc9f92/task_output/buildah-0.11-1.git6bad262.fc27.log).
It says it is supposed to be executable. I am not sure that's true.
This is `bash-completion`, it doesn't have to be executable. Am I wrong?

Sure, no problem! I will work on this in the following days 😄
No it should not need to be executable.
